oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Hungry, hangry or just off? Supportive care for the off-feed cow
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: McArt, Jessica A. A.
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: indigestion
rumen dysfunction
oral fluids
transfaunation
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Off-feed cows are among the most frequent and frustrating presentations in dairy practice. First, we must determine if decreased intake is the primary problem or a consequence of disease, and then we must deliver supportive care that aids in the restorations of rumen function and hydration. Supportive therapy is aimed at aimed at covering the bases of many poten­tial subclinical diseases that might affect rumen motility and function, including provision of Ca, Mg, P, K, Na and energy, and knowledge of how these aspects of oral fluid therapy can help restore rumen function in important for choosing the right product to administer. Consideration of water temperature and tonicity of orally administered fluids is central to successful therapy, and rumen transfaunation and access to hay should be considered based on available equipment and facilities.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; No. 1 (2026): 2026 Recent Graduate Conference Proceedings; 31-34
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
